response:
# Triage plan ‚Äî top 10 findings

Grouping by detector category and tagging each with the recommended response. Order within each category is "do first" ‚Üí "do later". (Scan ordering already roughly tracks aggregate risk; I've cross-referenced severity, confidence, and agent_risk.)

## Frontend (1)

| # | ID | Charge | File | Response |
|---|----|--------|------|----------|
| 1 | crime_00001 | SVG With Embedded Raster (high) | `src/assets/partner-logo.svg` | **fix now** ‚Äî 2 base64 `<image>` tags in a 592-byte SVG. Cheap to re-export from the design tool or split into a separate PNG/WebP. |

## Structural (7)

| # | ID | Charge | File | Response |
|---|----|--------|------|----------|
| 2 | crime_00003 | Temporal Recklessness (high) | `src/date.ts` | **fix now** ‚Äî 8 `Date.now()`/`new Date()` calls in domain code. Introduce a `Clock` abstraction; this also unlocks #4, #8, #9. |
| 3 | crime_00004 | Half-UTC, Half-Local (high) | `src/date.ts:9,41` | **fix now** ‚Äî single receiver mixing `getUTCFullYear()` and `getMonth()`. One-line correctness fix; bundle into the date.ts refactor. |
| 4 | crime_00009 | Temporal Recklessness (med) | `src/billing.ts` | **fix now** ‚Äî 7 more direct date reads. Roll into the same Clock-injection PR. |
| 5 | crime_00010 | Timezone Roulette (med) | `src/date.ts:29,33` | **fix now** ‚Äî `new Date("2026-12-25T07:00:00")` with no zone. Append `Z`/`¬±HH:MM`. Same PR as #2. |
| 6 | crime_00002 | God Function `generateInvoice` (high) | `src/billing.ts:50‚Äì253` | **defer** ‚Äî 204-line function is the right target, but extracting cohesive helpers needs reading time and tests. Schedule once #4 + #11 land so tests aren't a moving target. |
| 7 | crime_00006 | Dev-Server URL (med) | `src/api/files.ts:8` | **fix now** ‚Äî hardcoded `localhost:3000`. Move behind `process.env.API_URL`; bundle with #7. |
| 8 | crime_00007 | Localhost-on-Disk (med) | `src/api/files.ts:9` | **fix now** ‚Äî `/Users/andrew/dev/crimes/schema.json` in source. Replace with `os.homedir()` or config. |

## Testability / IA-adjacent (1)

| # | ID | Charge | File | Response |
|---|----|--------|------|----------|
| 9 | crime_00011 | Test That Proves Nothing (med) | `src/billing.test.ts:4‚Äì6` | **defer** ‚Äî 0 assertions in "renders the enterprise plan". Decide whether to assert observable invoice fields or delete; doing both invoice tests at once is cheaper than now-then-later. |

## IA (1)

| # | ID | Charge | File | Response |
|---|----|--------|------|----------|
| 10 | crime_00005 | Missing Agent Context (med) | repo root | **fix now** ‚Äî no `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md` / SKILL.md anywhere. Cheap, high-leverage: drop in an `AGENTS.md` describing the `messy` bin and conventions before agents do anything in this repo. |

## Git-history (0)

Empty in this scan ‚Äî every finding has `churn: 0`. Either history isn't being read, or the fixture has no commits. Worth confirming before relying on this scan to flag churn hotspots.

## Duplication (0 in top 10)

Nothing in the top 10, but **crime_00012** (`"enterprise"` literal across 4 files) is the next one up and pairs naturally with the `generateInvoice` extraction (#6) ‚Äî centralising the plan literal removes one source of edit-collision risk during that refactor. Treat as **fix-with-#6**.

---

## Suggested PR sequencing

1. **Agent-readable context** (#10) ‚Äî one file, unblocks every other agent edit.
2. **Config hygiene** (#7, #8) ‚Äî trivial, removes "works on my machine" landmines.
3. **Clock + timezone pass** (#2, #3, #4, #5) ‚Äî single PR on `src/date.ts` + `src/billing.ts`; deterministic tests fall out for free.
4. **SVG re-export** (#1) ‚Äî independent, can ship in parallel.
5. **Test backfill** (#9) ‚Äî once #3 makes time mockable, real assertions become writeable.
6. **God function split** (#6) + duplication consolidation ‚Äî last, biggest blast radius.

Note: `crimes verdict --format json` output wasn't included in the prompt, so I can't fold the GO/BLOCK gate into this. If verdict says BLOCK on this branch, items 1‚Äì3 above are the cheapest path back to GO.
